retrieved_docs.title: Falls and Fall Prevention in Older Adults -- Toxicokinetics
retrieved_docs.content: Cysteine (Cys) is a non-essential amino acid, sulfur-containing, HOOCCH (NH2) CH2SH; it can be derived by biosynthesis from serine or methionine after it has been transformed into cystathionine. One of its deficiencies leads to muscle weakness and imbalances in protein synthesis. Cysteine is essential (together with glycine and glutamic acid) for the synthesis of glutathione (GSH); the latter is fundamental for the detoxification of the metabolites of paracetamol. In elderly people with chronic pain, taking acetaminophen is very frequent; according to studies, the depletion of GSH and cysteine to clean the liver site causes sarcopenia in the long run.

retrieved_docs.title: Odds Ratio -- Function -- Odds Ratio Confidence Interval
retrieved_docs.content: To answer if this finding is significant, the confidence interval is calculated.  The confidence interval gives an expected range for the true odds ratio for the population to fall within.  If estimating the odds of lung cancer in smokers versus non-smokers of the general population based on a smaller sample, the true population odds ratio may be different than the odds ratio found in the sample.  In order to calculate the confidence interval, the alpha, or our level of significance, is specified. An alpha of 0.05 means the confidence interval is 95% (1 – alpha) the true odds ratio of the overall population is within range. A 95% confidence is traditionally chosen in the medical literature (but other confidence intervals can be used). The following formula is used for a 95% confidence interval (CI). Upper 95% CI = e ^ [ln(OR) + 1.96 sqrt(1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d)] Lower 95% CI = e ^ [ln(OR) - 1.96 sqrt(1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d)]

retrieved_docs.title: Falls and Fall Prevention in Older Adults -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Normal gait results from effective coordination of the following neural components: basal ganglia brainstem system, regulated muscle tone, and functional processing of sensory information such as vision, hearing, and proprioception. The risk of falling is increased in older adults because (1) these functions decline with age (2) the probability of accumulating medical issues increases with age, and (3) associated medications are often increased as well. With aging usually comes a wide-based gait, along with a decrease in gait velocity, step length, and lower limb strength. A fall most often results from interactions between these long-term predisposing factors and short-term predisposing environmental factors such as an adverse drug reaction, acute illness, or a trip on an irregular surface.

retrieved_docs.title: Falls and Fall Prevention in Older Adults -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Basically, the phenomenon of sarcopenia begins from the 4th decade, to arrive at a 50% muscular loss (inactive older individuals) in those aged 80 or older. The causes are different: decrease in anaerobic or white fibers; decline in protein synthesis (maintenance or hypertrophy), increase in connective tissue and fat within muscle fibers, mitochondrial alteration and increase in free radicals (ROS), increase in an inflamed cell environment, neurological, central and peripheral remodeling.

retrieved_docs.title: National Guidelines for Physical Activity -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Research has demonstrated that insufficient physical activity in all age groups not only increases all-cause mortality, cancer risk, serious falls, depression, and cognitive impairment but also contributes to metabolic disorders such as diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and dyslipidemia. Guidelines and strategies are needed to encourage people of all ages to intensify their level of physical activity.

retrieved_docs.content: Gender: In most older individuals, women fall more often than men

retrieved_docs.title: Localized Prostate Cancer -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Low risk: PSA less than 10 ng/ml and stage T1-T2a and grade group 1 High risk: PSA less than 20 or grade group 4-5 Intermediate risk: constitutes a variety of combinations falling between low and high risk

retrieved_docs.title: Falls and Fall Prevention in Older Adults -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Postprandial hypotension is a non-physiological reason that causes falls in older adults subjects, probably due to an autonomic system dysfunction or the declining function of the cardiovascular system. Obesity in older adults is another cause linked to the increase in falls, probably due to a further decline in muscle mass and neuromuscular function. Osteoporosis can cause rupture of the femoral neck in older adults and this event can often confuse the providers, particularly when the patient is uncooperative.

retrieved_docs.title: Falls and Fall Prevention in Older Adults -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Over 30% of individuals who are over the age of 65 fall every year. In approximately half of the cases, the falls are recurrent. This percentage increases to around 40% in individuals aged 85 years and above. [3] Approximately 10% of falls result in serious injuries, including fracture of the hip, other fractures, traumatic brain injury, or subdural hematoma. [4] [5] Falls are the most common type of accidents in people 65 years of age and older, and are the major cause of hospitalization related to injury in this age group. Injuries that are caused by falls are associated with increased mortality. Associated use of ambulance services, social care, and hospital care results in substantial financial costs. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Vitamin D Deficiency -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Although numerous observational studies suggest a potential link between vitamin D and various extraskeletal outcomes, the causal relationship remains unclear. Large randomized clinical trials, including VITAL, ViDA, and D2d, with over 30,000 participants combined, showed that vitamin D supplementation in adults replete with vitamin D (baseline serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D >50 nmol/L) does not prevent cardiovascular events, cancer, and type 2 diabetes mellitus or reduce mortality. [41] [42] [43] [44] [45] Recent trials have similarly failed to demonstrate vitamin D's benefit in preventing falls in older adults. [46] [47] [48]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 1885
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions represent a broad clinical category that includes specific bladder disorders (اضطراب في المثانة). These nonspecific genitourinary presentations frequently co‑occur with renal calculi (حصاة حالبية) and are clinically associated with prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا). Recognizing that bladder disorders fall under this umbrella, and that patients presenting with such symptoms may also have urinary stones or underlying prostate malignancy, helps clinicians pursue appropriate diagnostic evaluations and tailor management strategies for comprehensive urologic care.

retrieved_docs.content: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to lymph nodes of neck is classified as a specific form of نقائل إلى الرقبة (neck metastases), indicating that this particular cancer spread to the cervical lymph nodes falls under the broader category of metastases to the neck. This relationship underscores the importance of recognizing cervical lymph‑node involvement as a distinct manifestation of neck metastasis in oncologic evaluation and patient care.

This relationship indicates that these specific cancers fall under the broader category of malignant tumors, sharing the characteristic of uncontrolled, invasive cell growth. Recognizing them as forms of ورم صماوي خبيث helps guide diagnostic evaluation, treatment planning, and prognostic assessment.

retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions frequently co‑occur with حصاة حالبية (ureteral stone), سرطـان البروستاتا النَّقيلي (metastatic prostate cancer), and فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد (benign prostatic hyperplasia). This pattern of co‑occurrence indicates that patients who present with nonspecific genitourinary complaints may also have underlying stone disease, advanced prostate malignancy, or benign prostatic enlargement, underscoring the need for thorough diagnostic assessment to identify and manage these potentially related conditions.

retrieved_docs.content: Other male genital disorders are considered a subcategory of prostate disorder (اضطراب بروستاتي) and are linked to the practice of screening (التحصّي). Recognizing that other male genital disorders fall under the broader category of prostate disorder and are associated with screening emphasizes the importance of appropriate diagnostic evaluation in the management of these conditions.

retrieved_docs.content: The community describes a network of genitourinary disorders and interventions centered on the prostate and bladder. Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions are also clinically linked to prostate cancer, highlighting the association between nonspecific urinary presentations and malignant prostate disease. Therapeutic procedures targeting the male genital tract include Prostatolithotomy, Prostatotomy and calculus removal, categorized as other therapeutic procedures; male genital. retrieved_docs.content: The metastatic malignant neoplasm to the frontal bone is classified as a malignant neoplasm of the frontal bone, indicating that a cancerous tumor that has spread to the frontal bone falls within the broader category of primary malignant tumors arising in that bone.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 1234
retrieved_docs.content: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to temporal bone is classified as a malignant neoplasm of temporal bone (diagnosis), indicating that this metastatic cancer specifically involves the temporal bone. The broader category cancer of bone and connective tissue encompasses bone tumors, referred to in Arabic as ورم العظام, and is directly mapped to the diagnosis of malignant neoplasm of temporal bone. This relationship shows that metastatic malignant neoplasm to the temporal bone falls within the larger group of bone and connective‑tissue cancers, linking a specific temporal‑bone malignancy to the general classification of bone tumors.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1926
retrieved_docs.content: Prostata is described as having the location identified as اضطراب بروستاتي, indicating that prostatic disorders are situated within the prostate. Other male genital disorders are located in the prostata, are classified as a subcategory under اضطراب بروستاتي (having اضطراب بروستاتي as their parent), and are associated with التحصّي, suggesting a link to preventive screening or immunization measures. Recognizing that other male genital disorders arise in the prostate, fall under the broader category of prostatic disorder, and are connected with التحصّي informs clinical assessment, diagnosis, and preventive care strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2865
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions are frequently observed together with حصاة حالبية, indicating that patients presenting with vague or nonspecific genitourinary complaints may also have ureteral stones. In addition, these genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions are clinically associated with سرطان البروستاتا, suggesting that prostate cancer can manifest with or be accompanied by such nonspecific genitourinary findings. The same category of genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions also co‑occurs with سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي, reflecting that metastatic prostate cancer may present with similar ambiguous genitourinary manifestations. Recognizing these overlapping relationships is important for clinicians to consider a broad differential diagnosis, ensure appropriate imaging and laboratory evaluation, and promptly identify underlying urolithiasis or prostate malignancy, including its metastatic form.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2815
retrieved_docs.content: Urogenital disorders (اضطراب بولي تناسلي) encompass a range of conditions affecting the urinary and reproductive systems, and a urogenital fistula (ناسور بولي تناسلي) is a specific manifestation that falls within this broader category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2905
retrieved_docs.content: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to the thymus is a specific subtype of metastatic malignant neoplasm to endocrine glands, indicating that cancer spread to the thymus falls within the broader category of endocrine gland metastases. This hierarchical relationship highlights that while the thymus is a distinct organ, its involvement by metastatic disease is considered part of the overall spectrum of endocrine gland metastasis, which is relevant for diagnostic classification and treatment planning.
